title: HUF Corpus Housecleaning — INCLUDE/STORE Assessment (March 24, 2026)
principle: Clean house, clean mind. Peterson: set your house in perfect order before you criticize the world.
method: Full filesystem scrape of all HUF directories. Every concept document read. Data files (CSVs, HDI JSONs) excluded. Each document assessed as INCLUDE (keep in active corpus) or STORE (archive — superseded, redundant, or historical only). Peter reviews STORE candidates before any action.

ground_truth_counts.case_studies_data: ~50 files (CSVs, HDIs, scripts — data, not concept docs)

root_files_assessment.description: 110 files in HUF/ root. This is the active working state.

INCLUDE_current_foundational.reason: Foundational axiom — sits beneath everything. INCLUDE.

INCLUDE_current_governance.reason: Can HUF stand? Three legs + conditional fourth. INCLUDE.
Corporate single source of truth. INCLUDE.

INCLUDE_current_evidence.reason: CURRENT EMBER analysis. Germany p=0.0016. INCLUDE.
CURRENT GDP analysis. Japan/China p-values. INCLUDE.
L2 vs TVD correction — methodology transition. INCLUDE.

INCLUDE_current_methodology.reason: Shannon vs participation ratio fix. INCLUDE.

INCLUDE_current_outreach.reason: CURRENT letter. Ready to send. INCLUDE.

STORE_superseded_versions.description: These files have been replaced by newer versions. The content is preserved in the current version. STORE candidates for Peter's review.

files.reason: Had seasonal confound. Corrected in v2, then v3.
Metric naming error. Corrected in v3.
Original questions. Superseded by full review.
Had radiation monitor confound. Resolved in revision.
Older collective report. March 22 is more recent and comprehensive.

STORE_candidates_for_peter_review.description: These files may be redundant or of uncertain current value. Peter to review before any action.

files.question: Is this meaningfully different from v1.0? Agent found identical header content. If so, one can be stored.
Was the reorg ever executed? If executed, this is historical. If still pending, INCLUDE.

files.reason: Source of truth for cross-AI coordination. INCLUDE.
Active briefing — collective review in progress. INCLUDE.
Analyzer payload — real computed data. INCLUDE.
Analyzer template — simulated but useful. INCLUDE.

corpus.verdict: ALL INCLUDE — these are the foundational reference books
corpus.note: 4 context books (audience editions), 4 math books (audience editions), 1 developer manual, 1 register packet, 1 scrutiny edition. These are the mathematical and theoretical foundation. All INCLUDE.

governance.note: Voting register, deployment strategy, explainer, operator decision log, personal plan, suggestion intake, state observer, package ZIP. All actively referenced in governance operations.

papers.verdict: ALL INCLUDE — these are submission-ready drafts
papers.note: 21 files including CBD submission, GEO BON application, IFAC/IEEE control systems paper, Ramsar technical committee submission, Horizon Europe case for support, NERC responsive mode, CoDa mathematical geosciences, dual-column prototype, Ramsar contact materials. These represent the publication pipeline. All INCLUDE.

pillars.verdict: ALL INCLUDE — these are the three-pillar documents
pillars.note: Sufficiency Frontier v3.6, Fourth Category v2.6, Triad Synthesis v1.6, plus supporting documents. The three pillars are the publication-track structure of HUF.

technical_notes.note: CL-01 through CL-05 architecture, methodology appendix, morphology test, system discriminator. These define the control stack.

wiki.note: Taxonomy, operator entry points, fourth category expanded, manufacturing applications. Quick-reference material.

case_studies.verdict: ALL INCLUDE — these are the validated domains
case_studies.note: Backblaze v3.0, Energy v1.0, Planck v1.0, Ramsar Croatia package, Toronto infrastructure. Five real-data case studies. The data files within (CSVs, HDIs) are raw data — keep as-is, they feed the analyses.

review_trace.note: 4 review cycles documenting collective AI verdicts. These are the evidence chain for how decisions were made. Provenance matters for publication.

archive.note: 50 files across 6 subdirectories. These are already archived — old drafts, old decision logs, old pillar versions, old provenance. The archive directory IS the store.

build_scripts.verdict: INCLUDE as infrastructure — no action needed
build_scripts.note: 66 build scripts documenting document construction. These are the build chain. Keep as-is.

summary.subdirectories: ALL INCLUDE — no subdirectory-level archival needed
summary.net_effect_if_stored: Root directory would go from 110 to 84 active files (26 superseded versions moved to archive/). This would reduce clutter significantly while preserving every current document.
summary.naming_note: The framework is HUF — Higgins Unity Framework. Some older documents use RSM (Ratio-State Monitoring) as the framework name. These are the same framework. RSM was the working name; HUF is the current name. No documents need renaming — the evolution is documented and historical references are valid.
summary.key_observation: The 26 STORE candidates are ALL older versions of documents that have current versions. No unique content would be lost. The version history is: Calibration Rack v1-v4 (v5 current), EMBER Drift v1-v2 (v3 current), Collective Next Steps v1-v5 (v6 current), Grok Integration v2-v3 (v4 current), Peterson Letter v1-v3.1 (v4 current), Outreach v1 (v2 current), plus a few single-version superseded files.

drift_corrections_applied_today: Parent directory analyzer count corrected from 2 to 3 (React .jsx was missed)
Total active artifacts corrected from 116 to 117
CLS sigmoid formulas stripped from GROK-INT-002 (open-loop compliance)
ISSUE-007 updated to PARTIALLY COMPLETE (Grok portion done)
peter_action_required: Review the 26 STORE candidates above. If approved, Claude will move them to the archive/ directory in a single operation. The 3 uncertain items need your decision — are the reorg documents still pending or historical?
